methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Deepnight is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Lucas Young and Thomas Li that has raised $5.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public peers in the same sector and stage cohort, including Series A-stage companies like Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M), as well as Seed-stage peer Tenna Systems ($60M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $5.5M raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $13.75M. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—spanning $60M to $500M with a median of $150M—suggests $150M. The disparity reflects the maturity gap between Deepnight's Seed stage and the Series A valuation anchor in the peer set.

The triangulated valuation range is $12M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $82M. This wide range reflects material uncertainty around product-market fit validation and competitive positioning within a crowded frontier-defense sector. The low-end estimate is more defensible given Deepnight's capital deployed to date; the high-end approach applies peer multiples that assume successful Series A execution. Key uncertainties include customer traction, technical differentiation within the peer set, and fundraising velocity assumptions embedded in the stage-progression method.
